commit_msg: docs(readme,director): simplify cli interface and enhance formatting
author: Omkar
title: CASE FILE — The Rewrite
subtitle: He cleaned up the crime scene. New words. Better formatting. Same blood on the walls.
when_where: ☀️ 3:15 PM, the afternoon was burning itself out. Still main. Still confessing in public. **Omkar opened the README and the director docs again—not to add new truth, but to make the old truth *readable*.** 📖 The crime scene needed better lighting, better prose. Presentation was everything.
who_whom: 🎨 The CLI interface and the narrative around it became his canvas. **He wasn't fixing the threading issue; he was framing it.** Simplify, enhance, format—the language of improvement masking the language of erasure. 💬 The docs would be so clean, so professional, that readers might miss the corpse underneath.
what_why: ✏️ He rewrote the story. Cleaner prose. Better structure. **The same dangerous code lived in the same dangerous branch, but now it wore a suit.** 🖋️ By enhancing the formatting and simplifying the interface description, he made the README *feel* authoritative, complete, solved. 🧩 The formatting changes were a kind of gaslighting—they suggested that if the docs looked good, the product must be good. **But the threading issues remained. They always do.**
verdict: He didn't fix the app; he just made the failure look more professional.
